rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CORA MARY SUTTON JACKSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/06/2021. 1) No courtesy copy of the petition and proposed orders have been received. 2) No DE-121 form is on file. 3) Item 3g(2) of the petition was completed, even though the petitioner is seeking appointment as successor executor. 4) The first box at item 3g(1)(d) was not checked. 5) Item 5a(7) or (8) must be checked. 6) Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents. 7) Has a personal representative been appointed for the estates of Essie Sutton, Percy Sutton, and Clifton Sutton? If not, successors in interest must be identified. 8) Please provide the names and addresses of the currently acting trustee(s) of the Cora Mary Sutton Jackson Revocable Appointment Trust 2000. If the petitioner is the sole trustee, the names and addresses of all beneficiaries of the trust must be provided. 9) Please provide a copy of a deed or other instrument showing how the decedent acquired title to the estate real property.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANNE J. BOOTH REVOCABLE TRUST DATED MARCH 6, 2015 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To Confirm Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/25/21. The wrong form of Notice of Hearing was served: DE-115 is required for a petition to determine claim to property. Probate Code Section 850 succeeded Heggstad and sets higher standards for proving that assets not transferred to a trust during a trustor'ss life were, in fact, intended to be held in the trust. In other words, a beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] A copy of each bank account statement immediately preceding the trustor'ss date of death must be filed to confirm how title to the accounts was vested. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 1/15/21. If the supplement cannot be filed in time to serve a copy of it [CRC 7.53(b)] or the heirs-at-law will not waive 30 days' personal service of notice of the petition and supplement, the hearing will need to be continued further.

rulings.Rulings: continued to 1/4/21. no file stamped copy of the petition and no proposed order delivered to the examiner as required by local rules. new deadline for providing both in order to avoid an automatic continuance is noon on wednesday, 12/30/20.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DARLENE FULLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate will be set for 6/21/2022. If a petition for final distribution (or a report of status with a petition for additional time to administer the estate) is not filed at least one week prior to 6/21/2022, an appearance will be required on 6/21/2022.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ADA TOM LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible due to the recent stay at home order issued by the mayor. Please review the 8th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (to be addressed at hearing): 1) Petition alleges Decedent's Will is Lost. Probate Code 6124. If the testator's will was last in the testator's possession, the testator was competent until death, and neither the will nor a duplicate original of the will can be found after the testator's death, it is presumed that the testator destroyed the will with intent to revoke it. This presumption is a presumption affecting the burden of producing evidence.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MERVYN F. BURKE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Matter will be called for admission of will. No appearance required.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARION JENKINS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 8th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 8th amended general order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MELVIN FLEMING
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/25/2021. Issues: 1) Karen Surim Esq. filed a family law form FL-950 in probate indicating noticed of limited scope representation that the attorney will represent the party at the 11/23/2020 hearing and for any continuance of that hearing. Please provide legal authority for using FL-950 in probate. 2) Please provide legal authority for Karen Suri Esq filling the Supplemental Information on behalf of Petitioner on 12/08/2020 when the filing does not appear to be covered by the Noticed of Limited Scope Representation and no substitution of attorney has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSE M. VILLALON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. close by 6/20/2022.
